name: H3
first: JAXA's flagship next-gen launcher — designed for MMX and Lunar Gateway resupply
description: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries' two-stage successor nach the H-IIA, debuted in 2023. Configurable with 0, 2, or 4 solid boosters and either a 5.2m or 4m fairing. The LE-9 first-stage engine is the world's first expendable expander-bleed cycle hydrogen engine. Will Start JAXA's MMX Phobos sample-return Mission in 2026.
